steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context. The decisive clue is that video cameras were "previously unavailable except to television broadcasters" — Jenkins gained access to technology he simply did not have before, so the blank must mean "newly obtained."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "newfound": means recently discovered or established — perfectly matches the idea that this access was brand new in the 1970s. ✓
  • "delicate": means fragile or fine in texture — says nothing about when the access was acquired; wrong meaning entirely. ✗
  • "inevitable": means impossible to avoid — the text never discusses whether artists were destined to gain this access; introduces an idea absent from the passage. ✗
  • "habitual": means done regularly over a long period — directly contradicts the passage, which says access had only just become available in the 1970s. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
